paper_title: Selection of carrier materials for formulation of the antagonistic Bacillus spp. against rice bacterial leaf blight

entities.description: A common rice disease caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae (Xoo) that reduces quality and yield.
The ability of certain bacteria to inhibit the growth of pathogens.
The study of diseases in plants, focusing on diagnosis, management, and control.
A bacterial strain used as a bio-control agent against rice bacterial leaf blight.
A bacterial strain evaluated for its antagonistic effects against rice pathogens.
A bacterial strain tested for formulation using various carrier materials.
A bacterial strain selected for its disease-reducing effects in rice.
A carrier material used for formulating Bacillus spp. to maintain cell density.
A natural carrier material evaluated for storing antagonistic bacteria.
A processing byproduct used as a solid carrier for microbial formulations.
A procedure used to examine viable cell densities in formulations monthly.
An in vitro test used to determine inhibition zones on agar plates.
The threshold of viable bacteria maintained in the carrier after six months.
The amount of rice production reduction caused by bacterial leaf blight.
Corresponding author and researcher at Can Tho University.

entities.id: Biotechnology Resesearch and Development Insitute, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic and research institution located in Vietnam.
